response: The West Indies coach seeks to optimize performance using a quadratic model for batting scores. To find the maximum score, we analyze the function \( S = 50 + 5n - 0.2n^2 \). This is a downward-opening parabola, so its vertex represents the maximum value. The vertex occurs at \( n = -b / (2a) \), where \( b = 5 \) and \( a = -0.2 \). Calculating this gives \( n = -5 / (2 \times -0.2) = 12.5 \). Since practice sessions must be whole numbers, we test integers 12 and 13. For \( n = 12 \), \( S = 50 + 60 - 28.8 = 81.2 \). For \( n = 13 \), \( S = 50 + 65 - 33.8 = 81.2 \). Both yield the same maximum average batting score of 81.2 runs. Thus, the optimal number of sessions is 12 or 13. Next, we evaluate the win probability change between 5 and 10 sessions. At \( n = 5 \), the score is \( S_5 = 50 + 25 - 5 = 70 \). The probability is \( P_5 = 1 / (1 + e^{-(0.1 \times 70 - 3)}) = 1 / (1 + e^{-4}) \). This equals approximately 0.9820. At \( n = 10 \), the score is \( S_{10} = 50 + 50 - 20 = 80 \). The probability is \( P_{10} = 1 / (1 + e^{-(0.1 \times 80 - 3)}) = 1 / (1 + e^{-5}) \). This equals approximately 0.9933. The change in win probability is \( 0.9933 - 0.9820 = 0.0113 \). This represents an increase of about 1.13 percentage points. The team benefits significantly from increased practice up to the peak. Strategic planning ensures resources are used efficiently for the best results. The mathematical model provides clear guidance for the coaching
